_doc: Round 34 / BC-06: per-gate applicability predicates. Each gate lists conjunctive predicates over the validated build_config; if every predicate evaluates true the gate applies to this fleet shape, otherwise it is reported as N/A by hil/dispatch.ps1 and the SIL gate. Op set: 'eq' (path == value), 'gt' (path > value), 'gte' (path >= value), 'truthy' (path is truthy). Empty 'applies_when' means the gate always applies. Source of truth: this file. Both dispatch.ps1 and tests/test_hil_dispatch_applicability_sil.py read it.

W4-01.title: Handheld E-stop latch
W4-01.rationale: Handheld E-stop gate is meaningless when no handheld is wired.

W4-02.title: Link-tune walk-down
W4-02.rationale: LoRa link-tune walk-down needs the handheld in the operator's hand.

W4-03.title: M7<->M4 watchdog trip
W4-03.rationale: M7+M4 are always present on the Portenta H7.

W4-04.title: Modbus disconnect -> E-stop
W4-04.rationale: Opta Modbus E-stop is core to every v25 build.

W4-05.title: Proportional ramp-out
W4-05.rationale: A bang-bang hydraulic build has no ramp curve to verify.

W4-06.title: Mixed-mode skip
W4-06.rationale: Mixed-mode requires at least one arm axis to mix with the tracks.

W4-07.title: Boot-PHY first-frame
W4-07.rationale: Boot-PHY ordering is a property of the radio stack; always applies.

W4-08.title: Camera keyframe round-trip
W4-08.rationale: No cameras -> no keyframe pipeline to verify.

W4-09.title: Async M7 TX IRQ
W4-09.rationale: M7 LoRa TX path is core; always applies.

W4-10.title: Fleet-key provisioning
W4-10.rationale: Fleet key provisioning is required regardless of optional features.
